 {"id":"beads_rust-3hws","title":"Study mcp_agent_mail codebase for agent-friendly error patterns","description":"# Study mcp_agent_mail for Agent-Friendly Error Patterns

## Purpose
Deep dive into /data/projects/mcp_agent_mail codebase to learn from its exemplary approach to agent communication, error handling, and intent correction. Apply these patterns to br's error handling and CLI output.

## Why mcp_agent_mail is a Master Class

Even though it's an MCP server (not a CLI), mcp_agent_mail demonstrates exceptional patterns for:

### 1. Deeply Insightful Error Messages
- Errors explain not just WHAT went wrong but WHY
- Context-aware suggestions based on the operation attempted
- Clear guidance on how to fix the issue

### 2. Agent Intent Recognition
- Understanding the 'legible intent' behind tool calls
- Seamlessly correcting minor mistakes when intent is clear
- Not failing pedantically when a reasonable interpretation exists

### 3. Helpful Warnings
- Proactive warnings about potential issues
- Suggestions for better approaches
- Validation feedback that educates rather than just rejects

## Research Tasks

### Phase 1: Codebase Exploration
- [ ] Map the error handling architecture
- [ ] Identify error message templates/patterns
- [ ] Document the intent-correction logic
- [ ] Note validation and warning patterns

### Phase 2: Pattern Extraction
- [ ] Catalog reusable error message patterns
- [ ] Document intent-matching heuristics
- [ ] Extract warning trigger conditions
- [ ] Identify agent-friendly output formats

### Phase 3: Application to br
- [ ] Map patterns to br's error types
- [ ] Design intent-correction for common br mistakes
- [ ] Plan warning system for br operations
- [ ] Update second-pzr (structured errors) with learnings

## Key Files to Study
```
/data/projects/mcp_agent_mail/
├── src/
│   ├── error/          # Error types and handling
│   ├── validation/     # Input validation patterns
│   ├── tools/          # Tool implementations with error handling
│   └── ...
```

## Example Patterns to Look For

### Intent Correction
```
Agent calls: send_message(to="BlueLake", ...)
But BlueLake doesn't exist, and "BlueRake" does
→ "Did you mean 'BlueRake'? Auto-correcting..."
vs pedantic: "Agent BlueLake not found" (fails)
```

### Contextual Errors
```
Instead of: "Invalid project_key"
Better: "Project '/data/foo' not found. 
         Did you run ensure_project() first?
         Available projects: ['/data/bar', '/data/baz']"
```

### Proactive Warnings
```
"Warning: You're sending to 15 recipients. 
 Consider using CC for FYI-only recipients."
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Comprehensive notes on mcp_agent_mail patterns
- [ ] Pattern catalog applicable to CLI tools
- [ ] Specific recommendations for br error handling
- [ ] Updates to second-pzr with concrete examples

## Deliverables
- Research notes document (.beads/MCP_AGENT_MAIL_PATTERNS.md)
- Updated error handling design for br

## Dependencies
- Informs: second-pzr (Structured JSON error output)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"ScarletAnchor","estimated_minutes":0,"created_at":"2026-01-16T19:17:29.375551374Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T20:20:10.115197832Z","closed_at":"2026-01-16T20:20:10.115197832Z","close_reason":"Created .beads/MCP_AGENT_MAIL_PATTERNS.md with 10+ patterns extracted from mcp_agent_mail: StructuredError class, intent detection (6 categories), O(1) validation, query sanitization, proactive warnings, and graceful defaults. Includes specific recommendations for br: Levenshtein ID suggestions, 'did you mean?' for status/type/priority, actionable hints in errors. All patterns have code citations from app.py, utils.py, config.py.","external_ref":"second-9fh","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"compacted_at_commit":"","original_size":0}
 {"id":"beads_rust-3j17","title":"EPIC: Interactive TUI Mode with Ratatui","description":"# Interactive TUI Mode with Ratatui

## Background & Rationale

Based on research of mature TUI tools (beads_viewer with Bubbletea, cass with Ratatui) and 2025-2026 terminal UI best practices, br would benefit from an optional interactive TUI mode for users who prefer visual exploration over command-line invocations.

### Why This Matters
- beads_viewer (bv) provides TUI for Go beads but requires separate installation
- Integrated TUI mode gives users choice without extra tools
- TUI enables rapid exploration of issue graphs and dependencies
- Visual feedback improves UX for complex operations
- Follows the dual-mode pattern: TUI for humans, CLI for agents

## Goals
Deliver an optional TUI mode using Ratatui that allows users to browse, filter, and manage issues interactively while maintaining the CLI-first design philosophy.

## In-Scope (v1 TUI)
- `br tui` or `br -i` to launch interactive mode
- Issue list view with filtering and sorting
- Issue detail view with full content
- Dependency graph visualization (ASCII art)
- Keyboard navigation (vim-style bindings)
- Search/filter as you type
- Quick actions (close, update status, add comment)
- Theme support (light/dark, custom colors)

## Out-of-Scope (v1)
- Full feature parity with bv
- Graph metrics (PageRank, betweenness) - use bv for that
- Real-time collaboration features
- Mouse support (keyboard-first)

## Technical Approach

### Ratatui Stack
```toml
[dependencies]
ratatui = "0.30"
crossterm = "0.28"
tui-textarea = "0.7"     # Text input
tui-tree-widget = "0.22" # Tree views
```

### Architecture (Elm-inspired)
```rust
struct App {
    state: AppState,
    issues: Vec<Issue>,
    selected: Option<usize>,
    filter: String,
    mode: Mode,
}

enum Mode {
    List,
    Detail,
    Search,
    Action,
}

enum Message {
    KeyPress(KeyEvent),
    IssueSelected(String),
    FilterChanged(String),
    ActionCompleted(Result<(), Error>),
}

fn update(app: &mut App, msg: Message) -> Option<Command> { ... }
fn view(app: &App) -> impl Widget { ... }
```

### Styling with Ratatui
```rust
use ratatui::style::{Color, Modifier, Style};

let priority_style = match issue.priority {
    0 => Style::default().fg(Color::Red).add_modifier(Modifier::BOLD),
    1 => Style::default().fg(Color::Yellow),
    2 => Style::default().fg(Color::White),
    _ => Style::default().fg(Color::DarkGray),
};
```

### Feature Flag
```toml
[features]
default = []
tui = ["ratatui", "crossterm", "tui-textarea"]
```

Build with TUI: `cargo build --features tui`

## Acceptance Criteria
- `br tui` launches interactive mode
- Issue list displays with priority colors
- Keyboard navigation works smoothly
- Filter/search is responsive
- Quick actions update database correctly
- Graceful exit restores terminal state
- Works on Linux, macOS, Windows Terminal

## Views

### List View
```
┌─ br - 24 issues ready ─────────────────────────────┐
│ Filter: [                    ] [↑↓] Navigate [q] Quit │
├────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ ● P0 beads_rust-8f8  EPIC: Port beads to Rust      │
│ ● P1 beads_rust-0v1  Sync safety hardening         │
│ ○ P1 beads_rust-1ce  Phase 3: Relations & Search   │
│ ○ P2 beads_rust-4z6  Colored Terminal Output       │
│   ...                                              │
├────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ [Enter] View  [c] Close  [u] Update  [/] Search    │
└────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

### Detail View
```
┌─ beads_rust-8f8 ───────────────────────────────────┐
│ EPIC: Port beads (SQLite+JSONL) to Rust as 'br'   │
│ Priority: P0 (Critical)  Status: OPEN  Type: epic │
│ Owner: jeff141421@gmail.com                        │
├────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ ## Description                                     │
│ Deliver a classic, non-invasive Rust port of bd   │
│ with full JSONL/SQLite parity and command...      │
│                                                    │
│ ## Children (5)                                    │
│   ✓ Phase 1: Foundation                           │
│   ✓ Phase 2: Core Commands                        │
│   ○ Phase 3: Relations & Search                   │
│   ...                                             │
├────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ [Esc] Back  [c] Close  [e] Edit  [d] Add dep      │
└────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

## References
- Ratatui: https://ratatui.rs/
- Ratatui Templates: https://github.com/ratatui/templates
- awesome-ratatui: https://github.com/ratatui/awesome-ratatui
- beads_viewer Bubbletea architecture
- cass TUI implementation

## Dependencies
- Phase 5: Polish & Conformance (for stable CLI foundation)
- Colored Terminal Output (shares color palette)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","estimated_minutes":0,"created_at":"2026-01-16T18:48:43.702596786Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T18:54:38.511912462Z","closed_at":"2026-01-16T18:54:38.511912462Z","close_reason":"ERROR: TUI mode is bv's domain. bv IS the TUI for beads. br is CLI-only.","external_ref":"second-9u2","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"compacted_at_commit":"","original_size":0}

 {"id":"beads_rust-3mg","title":"Feature: Project Scaffolding - Cargo.toml & Build System","description":"# Project Scaffolding (Cargo + Toolchain + Layout)

## Purpose
Establish the Rust project skeleton and dependency baseline for `br`, aligned with the classic beads spec (SQLite + JSONL, YAML config).

## Required Files
### Cargo.toml (baseline)
- Edition: 2024 (nightly toolchain)
- Binary: `br`
- **Required deps** (exact versions pinned):
  - CLI: `clap` (derive + env)
  - SQLite: `rusqlite` (bundled + modern_sqlite)
  - Serialization: `serde`, `serde_json`, **`serde_yaml`** (config.yaml)
  - Time: `chrono` (serde)
  - Hashing: `sha2`
  - Error handling: `anyhow`, `thiserror`
  - Logging: `tracing`, `tracing-subscriber`
  - Utilities: `once_cell`, `rayon`
  - Optional: `colored` (status icons), `indicatif`
- **Remove** TOML config assumptions; config is YAML.

### rust-toolchain.toml
- Nightly pin (per repo) + rustfmt/clippy.

### build.rs (optional)
- Build metadata (vergen-gix) for version/commit output.

### .cargo/config.toml
- Incremental builds, optional linker optimizations.

## Directory Layout (minimal)
```
src/
  main.rs
  lib.rs
  cli/
  model/
  storage/
  export/
  import/
  config/
  error/
  format/
  util/
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- `cargo check --all-targets` passes.
- `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings` passes.
- `cargo fmt --check` passes.
- Release profile matches AGENTS.md (opt-level z, lto, codegen-units=1, panic=abort, strip).","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"feature","estimated_minutes":0,"created_at":"2026-01-16T06:14:06.009993405Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T08:12:33.507519527Z","closed_at":"2026-01-16T08:12:33.507519527Z","close_reason":"Completed project scaffolding: rust-toolchain.toml, Cargo.toml with serde_yaml, src/ directory structure, minimal main.rs/lib.rs, all checks passing (cargo check, clippy, fmt)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"compacted_at_commit":"","original_size":0}
 {"id":"beads_rust-3nj1","title":"Integrate rich output into query command","description":"## Command: br query <run|list>

### Traffic Level: LOW
Custom SQL-like queries against issues.

### Current Implementation
Location: src/cli/commands/query.rs

### Integration Steps

#### query list
```
╭─ Saved Queries ─────────────────────────────────────╮
│                                                     │
│  Name           Description                         │
│  ─────────────────────────────────────────────────  │
│  my-issues      Issues assigned to me               │
│  p0-open        Open P0 issues                      │
│  stale-bugs     Bug issues not updated in 7 days    │
│                                                     │
╰─────────────────────────────────────────────────────╯
```

#### query run <NAME|EXPR>
Results displayed using IssueTable with query expression shown:
```
Query: status=open AND priority=P0

╭─ Results (3) ───────────────────────────────────────╮
│                                                     │
│ ID              Title                    Priority   │
│ ───────────────────────────────────────────────────│
│ beads_rust-abc1 Critical auth failure    P0         │
│ beads_rust-def2 Data corruption bug      P0         │
│ beads_rust-ghi3 Service outage           P0         │
│                                                     │
╰─────────────────────────────────────────────────────╯
```

### Testing Requirements

#### Unit Tests
```rust
#[cfg(test)]
mod tests {
    use super::*;

    #[test]
    fn test_query_list_formatting() {
        let queries = vec![
            SavedQuery { name: "my-issues".into(), description: "Issues assigned to me".into() },
            SavedQuery { name: "p0-open".into(), description: "Open P0 issues".into() },
        ];
        let ctx = OutputContext::rich();
        let output = format_query_list(&queries, &ctx);
        assert!(output.contains("my-issues"));
        assert!(output.contains("p0-open"));
    }

    #[test]
    fn test_query_results_header() {
        let query_expr = "status=open AND priority=P0";
        let results = vec![make_test_issue("test-1")];
        let ctx = OutputContext::rich();
        let output = format_query_results(query_expr, &results, &ctx);
        assert!(output.contains("Query:"));
        assert!(output.contains("status=open"));
    }

    #[test]
    fn test_query_empty_results() {
        let query_expr = "status=nonexistent";
        let results: Vec<Issue> = vec![];
        let ctx = OutputContext::rich();
        let output = format_query_results(query_expr, &results, &ctx);
        assert!(output.contains("0") || output.contains("No results"));
    }

    #[test]
    fn test_json_mode_query_list() {
        let queries = vec![
            SavedQuery { name: "test".into(), description: "Test query".into() },
        ];
        let ctx = OutputContext::json();
        let output = format_query_list(&queries, &ctx);
        let parsed: serde_json::Value = serde_json::from_str(&output).unwrap();
        assert!(parsed.is_array());
        assert_eq!(parsed[0]["name"], "test");
    }

    #[test]
    fn test_json_mode_query_results() {
        let query_expr = "status=open";
        let results = vec![make_test_issue("test-1")];
        let ctx = OutputContext::json();
        let output = format_query_results(query_expr, &results, &ctx);
        let parsed: serde_json::Value = serde_json::from_str(&output).unwrap();
        assert!(parsed["query"].is_string());
        assert!(parsed["results"].is_array());
    }
}
```

#### Integration Tests
```rust
#[test]
fn test_query_run_expression() {
    let (mut storage, dir) = test_db_with_dir();
    init_test_project(&dir);
    create_test_issue(&mut storage, "Open issue");

    let result = run_query(&dir, "status=open");
    assert!(result.is_ok());
    assert!(!result.unwrap().is_empty());
}

#[test]
fn test_query_list_shows_saved() {
    let (mut storage, dir) = test_db_with_dir();
    init_test_project(&dir);
    // Assuming saved queries are stored somewhere

    let result = run_query_list(&dir);
    assert!(result.is_ok());
}
```

#### E2E Test Script
```bash
#!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail
source "$(dirname "$0")/../common/test_helpers.sh"

log_info "=== E2E: Query Command ==="
setup_test_db

log_step "Initialize and create test data"
br init --prefix test
br create "Open issue" --priority P0
br create "Another issue" --priority P1

log_step "Testing query run"
QUERY_OUTPUT=$(br query run "priority=P0" 2>&1 || true)
log_debug "Query output: $QUERY_OUTPUT"
if echo "$QUERY_OUTPUT" | grep -qi "Open issue\|Results"; then
    log_pass "Query returned results"
else
    log_warn "Query output different than expected"
fi

log_step "Testing query list"
LIST_OUTPUT=$(br query list 2>&1 || true)
log_debug "List output: $LIST_OUTPUT"
# May show no saved queries, that's ok
log_pass "Query list executed"

log_step "Testing JSON output"
QUERY_JSON=$(br query run "priority=P0" --json 2>&1 || echo '{"results":[]}')
log_debug "JSON: $QUERY_JSON"
echo "$QUERY_JSON" | jq -e '.results'
log_pass "Query JSON valid"

log_pass "=== All query tests passed ==="
```

#### Logging Requirements
- Log query parsing: `debug!(expression, "Parsing query")`
- Log query execution: `trace!(result_count, "Query executed")`
- Log saved query lookup: `debug!(query_name, "Looking up saved query")`","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T20:37:03.614296060Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-20T20:05:35.434965555Z","closed_at":"2026-01-20T20:05:23.787079817Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-3nj1","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-31nl","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-08T22:47:30Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-3nj1","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-36dk","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-08T22:47:30Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":56,"issue_id":"beads_rust-3nj1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Implemented rich output for query command.
